topic: Religious & Ethical Teachings about Crime & Punishment
subject: religious-studies

items.q: Is the Buddha a god in Buddhism?
items.correct: No — the Buddha (Siddhartha Gautama) is regarded as an enlightened human teacher who discovered the path to liberation. Theravada Buddhism is generally non-theistic; some Mahayana traditions develop more elaborate cosmologies.
items.misconception: Buddhists worship the Buddha as their god.
items.why: Statues and offerings look like worship to students, but classically the Buddha is venerated as teacher, not worshipped as creator god. The path he taught, not his divinity, is what matters in most Buddhist traditions.

items.q: What does Buddhism teach about suffering?
items.correct: All existence involves dukkha (suffering/unsatisfactoriness), caused by tanha (craving and attachment); ending craving leads to liberation from suffering.
items.misconception: Buddhism teaches that life is nothing but pain and misery.
items.random_wrong: Buddhism teaches that suffering is caused by demons.
items.why: Dukkha is broader than pain — it includes dissatisfaction and impermanence. Buddhism doesn't say life is misery; it says even pleasure carries inherent unsatisfactoriness because it ends.

items.q: What does the teleological (design) argument claim?
items.correct: Apparent purpose, order or complexity in the universe implies an intelligent designer — Paley's watchmaker analogy is the classic example.
items.misconception: The design argument proves God created the world in seven days.
items.random_wrong: The design argument proves evolution is false.
items.why: The argument is philosophical, not anti-evolutionary. Many religious thinkers accept evolution as the designer's mechanism — students conflate design with young-earth creationism.

items.q: When was UK capital punishment abolished?
items.why: It's 1965. “1948” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In Matthew 18:22, Jesus tells Peter to forgive 'seventy times seven'. What number is seven multiplied by seventy?
items.why: The real figure is 490 times. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
